remember: Students will be able to recall the historical background of the Olympic Movement.
Students will be able to list the key milestones in the development of the Olympic Movement.
Students will be able to identify the main principles of the Olympic Movement.
understand: Students will be able to explain the significance of the Olympic Movement in promoting international understanding and friendship.
Students will be able to describe the structure and functions of the International Olympic Committee (IOC).
Students will be able to analyze the role of Pierre de Coubertin in the revival of the Olympic Games.
apply: Students will be able to apply the principles of the Olympic Movement in their own sports and physical activities.
Students will be able to use the Olympic ideals to promote fair play and sportsmanship in their school and community.
Students will be able to design a campaign to promote the Olympic Movement in their own school or community.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the impact of the Olympic Movement on international relations and global understanding.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of the Olympic Movement in promoting youth development and physical education.
Students will be able to compare the Olympic Movement with other international sports organizations.

ncert_objectives: Students will understand the concept of the Olympic Movement and its historical background.
Students will be able to explain the significance of the Olympic Movement in promoting international understanding and friendship.
Students will analyze the role of Pierre de Coubertin in the revival of the Olympic Games.
board_exam_objectives: Students will be able to describe the history and development of the Olympic Movement.
Students will be able to explain the principles and objectives of the Olympic Movement.
Students will analyze the impact of the Olympic Movement on international relations and global understanding.
